items.description: Import path missing or dependency not installed.
items.fix: Verify file path or install missing dependency.

items.description: Prisma client not generated or output path mismatched.
items.fix: Run prisma generate and confirm output path.

items.description: Tailwind config mismatch or wrong version syntax.
items.fix: Verify Tailwind version and update @apply usage.

items.description: Wrong root directory or build command skipped real build.
items.fix: Check project root and build command configuration.

items.description: Build output directory not produced or misconfigured.
items.fix: Confirm framework preset and output directory.

items.description: Auth runtime missing required environment variable.
DB connection or schema mismatch at runtime.

items.fix: Ensure build output is included in deployment.

items.pattern: Type error: Route .* does not match
items.description: Next.js App Router page/layout type mismatch after upgrade.
items.fix: Update page component signature to match expected Next.js types.

items.description: Server-only import used in client component.
items.fix: Add 'use server' directive or move import to server component.

items.description: Serverless function exceeded execution time limit.
items.fix: Check for missing await, large DB queries, or increase timeout in vercel.json.

items.description: Package uses Node.js APIs not available in Edge Runtime.
items.fix: Switch to nodejs runtime in route config or replace incompatible package.

items.description: Database table missing — migration not applied or wrong schema.
items.fix: Run pending migrations or verify DATABASE_URL points to correct database.

items.description: NextAuth session token invalid or NEXTAUTH_SECRET changed.
items.fix: Verify NEXTAUTH_SECRET matches across environments. Clear cookies if rotated.

items.description: npm install fails due to incompatible package version requirements.
items.fix: Check which packages conflict, pin compatible versions, or use --legacy-peer-deps as last resort.

items.description: git apply rejects an LLM-generated unified diff. Hunk content is usually correct but the @@ -A,B +C,D @@ header line counts are wrong (every frontier LLM gets this arithmetic flaky).
items.fix: Apply patches with git apply --recount — the official git flag that recomputes hunk counts from actual content and ignores the header numbers. Use for both --check and apply steps.

items.description: npm install fails because a published monorepo package still has workspace:* in its dependencies. Occurs when the package was published with npm publish instead of pnpm publish — pnpm rewrites workspace:* to concrete versions on upload, npm does not.
items.fix: Publish workspace packages with pnpm publish --access public (never npm publish). Recover from a bad release by bumping version and republishing via pnpm; deprecate the broken version with npm deprecate <pkg>@<ver> <reason>.

items.pattern: pathspec .* did not match any files
items.description: git add fails right after a successful git apply. Usually the worker claimed to create a new file but the new-file hunk (@@ -0,0 +1,N @@) did not actually materialise on disk — a known sharp edge when combining git apply --recount with new-file creation hunks.
items.fix: Use git add -A after git apply instead of pathspec-based git add -- <files>. -A stages exactly what the apply materialised, ignoring anything the worker claimed to have touched but did not.
